questions.question: What is the term used to describe the brute force algorithm used to solve the Traveling Salesman Problem?
questions.explanation: Exhaustive Search is a brute force algorithm that involves checking all possible solutions to find the optimal one.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of the Exhaustive Search algorithm for the Traveling Salesman Problem?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of the Exhaustive Search algorithm for the Traveling Salesman Problem is O(n!), where n is the number of cities.

questions.question: What is the main disadvantage of using the Exhaustive Search algorithm for the Traveling Salesman Problem?
options: It is too slow for large inputs
It is too complex to implement
questions.explanation: The main disadvantage of using the Exhaustive Search algorithm for the Traveling Salesman Problem is that it is too slow for large inputs due to its exponential time complexity.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Annexure-II in the context of the Traveling Salesman Problem?
options: To provide a formula for the optimal solution
To describe the brute force algorithm
To outline the limitations of the Exhaustive Search algorithm
questions.explanation: Annexure-II is used to provide a detailed description of the brute force algorithm used to solve the Traveling Salesman Problem.

questions.question: Which section of Chapter 3 in the textbook discusses the Exhaustive Search algorithm in more detail?
questions.explanation: Section 3.4 of Chapter 3 in the textbook discusses the Exhaustive Search algorithm in more detail, including its time complexity and limitations.
